prompt_text: Open source or affordable HR tools instead of 15Five
response:
If you’re looking to replace **15Five** with something **open source or much more affordable**, your best options fall into two buckets: (1) broader open‑source HR suites that include performance/OKRs/engagement, and (2) focused, low‑cost SaaS tools that do just the “15Five‑like” bits (check‑ins, feedback, goals).

Below is a concise, practical shortlist.

---

## 1. Open‑source HR suites (free, self‑host or low-cost cloud)

These are full HRMS/HCM platforms, not just performance tools, but several include **performance management, reviews, goals, and basic engagement**.

### Frappe HR (HR + Payroll + performance)

- **Type:** 100% **open source** HR & payroll suite.[6]  
- **Cost:** Free if self‑hosted; paid but **“affordable & inclusive”** managed cloud, with costs not scaling linearly per user.[6]  
- **Scope:** End‑to‑end HR (recruitment → onboarding → leaves → payroll → exits) from **one dashboard and mobile app**.[6]  
- **Why as a 15Five alternative:**  
  - Has **employee management**, **leave**, **attendance**, and **payroll** out of the box.[1][6]  
  - Includes performance‑related workflows (appraisals, goals) within a broader HR stack (you’d handle weekly/bi‑weekly check‑ins via forms/Workflows or custom doctype if needed).  
- Good fit if you’re willing to self‑host or want a **single system for HR + performance + payroll** rather than a narrow check‑in tool.

### Horilla HRMS

- **Type:** **Open‑source HRMS**, built with Django.[2][7]  
- **Cost:** **Free and open source**, self‑host; also has a cloud version if you don’t want to run servers.[2][7]  
- **Scope:** Recruitment, onboarding, **employee management**, **attendance**, **leave**, **performance management**, payroll, asset management, and more.[7]  
- **Why as a 15Five alternative:**  
  - Native **performance management** module plus core HR.  
  - You can design **routine check‑in workflows** (weekly updates, 1:1 templates) via built‑in features or small customizations.  
- Good if you want **no license fees**, are OK with self‑hosting, and want a modern open‑source HR with performance built in.

### OrangeHRM Starter (open source edition)

- **Type:** Long‑running **free, open‑source HR software**.[3]  
- **Cost:** **Free for life** for the Starter open‑source version; you can self‑host or use cloud.[3]  
- **Scope:** Core HR, leave, time, basic performance modules, etc., designed for small and growing businesses.[3]  
- **Why as a 15Five alternative:**  
  - Gives you a **solid HR foundation** with employee records, leave, and basic performance tracking.[3]  
  - Less out‑of‑the‑box on “continuous feedback” than 15Five; think more structured reviews than weekly reflections.  
- Good if you want something **proven and stable** with a big user base (5M+ users).[3]

### Open HRMS

- **Type:** **Open‑source HR management** solution.[8]  
- **Cost:** Free to use and modify (typically Odoo‑based modules).[8]  
- **Scope:** Aims to cover **all HR processes**, for small to enterprise organizations, across industries.[8]  
- **Why as a 15Five alternative:**  
  - With the right modules, you can handle **appraisals, goals, and employee engagement** inside the HR stack.  
- Good if you already use **Odoo** or are comfortable with modular, extensible systems.

### MintHCM

- **Type:** **Open‑source HCM**, AI‑enabled.[4]  
- **Cost:** Free and open source; only your own server costs.[4]  
- **Scope:** Supports the **extended employee journey** (recruitment, development, performance, etc.).[4]  
- **Why as a 15Five alternative:**  
  - Focuses on human capital processes, including development and performance; you can model continuous feedback and goals here.  
- Good if you want a **people‑development‑oriented** open‑source suite, not just admin HR.

---

## 2. Other notable open‑source HR tools (for more modular setups)

From a curated list of top open‑source HRMS tools:[5]

- **iceHRM** – Multiple editions with source code; covers core HR with some performance and leave features
